proven.claim: PROVEN: algorithm returns true when ancestor is exactly 1 hop above node
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 353
; PROVEN: algorithm returns true when ancestor is exactly 1 hop above node
; Transition: iteration 1 checks node_id==ancestor_id → miss;
;             iteration 2 checks parent_id==ancestor_id → hit → return true

(declare-const node_id Int)
(declare-const parent_id Int)
(declare-const ancestor_id Int)

; Well-formed AST: unique IDs per node
(assert (not (= node_id parent_id)))

; ancestor IS the parent of node
(assert (= parent_id ancestor_id))

; Algorithm result: loop fires on iteration 2 → returns true
(declare-const result Int) ; 1=true 0=false
(assert (= result 1))

; Negate the property to prove it
(assert (not (= result 1)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — proves correct true-positive for 1-hop ancestry

proven.claim: PROVEN: algorithm returns false when ancestor ID appears in no node in the chain
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 353
; PROVEN: algorithm returns false when ancestor ID appears in no node in the chain
; Transition: 2-hop chain [node → root]; ancestor_id matches neither

(declare-const node_id Int)
(declare-const root_id Int)
(declare-const ancestor_id Int)

; Unique IDs; ancestor not in chain
(assert (not (= node_id root_id)))
(assert (not (= node_id ancestor_id)))
(assert (not (= root_id ancestor_id)))

; Algorithm: node → miss, root → miss, null → return false
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= result 0))

; Negate the property
(assert (not (= result 0)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — proves correct false for non-ancestors

proven.claim: PROVEN: loop terminates — depth strictly decreases by 1 each iteration
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; LINE: 353
; PROVEN: loop terminates — depth strictly decreases by 1 each iteration
; Transition: current = current.parent reduces depth by exactly 1

(declare-const depth_initial Int)
(declare-const k Int)
(declare-const depth_at_step_k Int)

; Preconditions: non-negative depth; k steps taken within valid range
(assert (>= depth_initial 0))
(assert (>= k 0))
(assert (<= k depth_initial))

; Transition: after k parent-pointer advances, depth = depth_initial - k
(assert (= depth_at_step_k (- depth_initial k)))

; Property: depth never goes negative while k ≤ depth_initial
(assert (< depth_at_step_k 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — loop terminates after at most depth_initial+1 iterations

violations.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION: isDescendantOf(x, x) returns true,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 353
; REACHABLE VIOLATION: isDescendantOf(x, x) returns true,
; contradicting strict "isDescendantOf" semantics
; (a node is NOT its own descendant under strict tree ancestry)
;
; Code transition: current starts at node; first check is current.id === ancestor.id.
; When both arguments refer to the same node (same id), this fires immediately.

(declare-const node_id Int)
(declare-const ancestor_id Int)

; Code path: same AST node passed as both arguments
(assert (= node_id ancestor_id))

; Algorithm transition: iteration 1 — current.id = node_id = ancestor_id → match → return true
(declare-const algorithm_result Int)
(assert (= algorithm_result 1))

; Strict "isDescendantOf" contract: self is NOT a descendant of self → must return 0
(declare-const strict_expected Int)
(assert (= strict_expected 0))

; Violation: implementation returns 1, strict contract demands 0
(assert (not (= algorithm_result strict_expected)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — confirms semantic mismatch is reachable
; Witness: any node_id s.t. node_id = ancestor_id
;          algorithm_result=1, strict_expected=0, (not (= 1 0)) holds
